canonical_run.dataset: MIMIC-IV Clinical Database Demo v2.2 (open access, ODbL)
canonical_run.cohort: first ICU stay per hospital admission
canonical_run.label: in-hospital mortality (hospital_expire_flag)
canonical_run.protocol: metrics averaged over 40 seed repeats of pooled out-of-fold 5-fold cross-validation

reproducer_targets.note: Numbers are the calibrated metrics on the 128-admission demo cohort. The demo is small, so single-split metrics are unstable; the cross-validated, multi-seed protocol above is what these targets and their tolerances describe. Demo numbers do NOT match the full-corpus figures below.

credentialed_public_method.note: LightGBM + Beta calibration on the full ~546K MIMIC-IV admissions, public-method pipeline only (no proprietary components). Requires PhysioNet MIMIC-IV credentialed access; run `python run.py --credentialed` with MIMIC_IV_CREDENTIALED=1 and MIMIC_IV_PATH set. The target is unpinned: expected range 0.80-0.88 based on published 24h-window MIMIC mortality models (Harutyunyan 2019, Bhattacharya 2020); it will be pinned in a future release once reproduced on at least 3 independent credentialed runs. Until then this tier's runs are informational and not gated.

changelog.change: Removed the leaked n_diagnoses feature (discharge-coded diagnosis count of the current admission, unknowable at the 24h prediction point) and replaced it with charlson_history, a Charlson comorbidity index (Quan 2005 code sets, Charlson 1987 weights) computed only from the subject's prior admissions discharged before the current admit time. Re-locked demo targets against the fixed 40-seed x 5-fold protocol.

internal_reference.note: The full internal MIMIC-IV pipeline (546K admissions, credentialed access) achieves the figures below with additional risk-stratification and calibration components beyond the LightGBM + Beta calibration reproduced here. They are recorded for reference and are not directly reproducible in this repository, which commits no credentialed data; see data/mimic_iv/README.md for the credentialed path.
